untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of: (1) initial breakout and sustained holding outside pre-breakout range, (2) pullback formation identification, (3) sharp reversal through pullback with loss of conviction, and (4) precise measurement of pullback extreme for stop placement and original breakout move for target sizing. These multi-bar pattern formations and dynamic threshold calculations cannot be expressed using only single-bar price/indicator comparisons with the allowed operators. The signal also specifies [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] placeholders for stop loss and target distances that require author-defined parameters not provided.
translation_notes: This failed breakout reversion signal describes a complex multi-stage pattern recognition process: (a) confirm initial breakout holds, (b) identify pullback formation, (c) detect sharp reversal closing back through pullback, (d) confirm return to original breakout level. While Donchian channels could approximate breakout/pullback extremes, they cannot capture the sequential logic of conviction loss and re-engagement. The pattern also requires measuring specific percentages of the original move for targeting, which needs ATR or range-based scaling unavailable in the provided indicator set. Author must provide concrete thresholds for stop loss placement (beyond pullback extreme) and target calculation (% of original breakout move).
